steps.title: Find an alternate cause
steps.content: The student's conclusion is causal: similarity drives same-brand cross-category purchasing. To weaken it, a finding should suggest another factor that could explain why high-similarity pairs had more same-brand purchases. That alternative must be tied to the categories themselves, because the observed pattern compares product-category pairs rather than individual shoppers' stated preferences.

steps.title: Use the confound
steps.content: A's price pattern does not explain the purchasing correlation. B restates the original pattern. D shows the pattern is equally strong across brand-recognition levels, which supports it. Competition changes purchasing opportunities across categories, so few competing products in high-similarity categories offers an alternate cause. The answer is C.
